question.text: In the Time Value of Money concept, the Present Value of a future cash flow will be HIGHER when:

answer.text: The discount rate is lower and the time period is shorter
Both discount rate and time period are higher

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Present Value = Future Value / (1+r)^n. PV increases when the discount rate (r) decreases or the time period (n) decreases — less discounting means higher present value. **The Trap:** Higher discount rate and longer time period both REDUCE present value by increasing the denominator. **Cross-Exam Context:** TVM calculations appear in Series X-A, X-B, VIII (Options pricing), and IV (Bond pricing) exams. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Use TVM to show clients the real cost of delaying investments — even a 5-year delay in starting SIPs can significantly reduce the terminal corpus due to compounding.
